Tum_All-
Stromal Tum_Ki67+ Tum_pRb
Tum_CCND1+ Myeloids Tum_All-
Stromal RCNs (stroma at the TSI)

Tumor All-

6 columns created for the exploration of ROIs containing TumAll- cells, aiming to distinguish the ROIs having the Tum_All- cells and lacking them. 
3 columns to choose the ROIs where the either of first 1,2 or 3 most prevalent RCNs in the ROI start with TumAll-


3 columns choosing the ROIs where the either of first 1,2 or 3 most prevalent RCNs in the ROI contain the RCN with TumAll-

Tum_CCND1+_Myeloids_Tum_All-/Stromal Tum_Ki67+ Tum_pRb

Choosing the ROIs with RCNs which have the prognostic contribution, linked to presence of CCND1 and Ki67
Checking if any of the RCN annotations (from RCN1 to RCN7) in the ROIs have the prognostic annotations.

ROIs depicting the TSI

Checking if any of the RCNs annotations in the given ROI containing the RCN starting with “Stroma”

TumAll- having ROIs

Columns created by choosing the RCNs having the TumAll- on the first place didn’t output the conclusive DEG results. 
Continued with columns created based on whether the most prevalent RCNs contained the TumAll- at all.

DEG analysis

DEG analysis

Interesting top hits

DLC1 - tumor suppressor that encodes a RhoGAP protein, which inactivates RhoA/B/C GTPases. By suppressing cytoskeletal remodeling and adhesion, it reduces cell motility, proliferation, and survival signaling—thereby inhibiting cancer progression.

SIGLEC-15 - immune modulator. On macrophages/myeloid cells could suppress T cell responses. Upregulated in variety of human cancers

DDX53 - promotes stem cell-like properties, autophagy, and confers resistance to anti-cancer drugs in breast cancer cells. Involved in RNA metabolism, such as splicing, translation, and stability. May regulate cell cycle and proliferation pathways. Functions are not fully understood but linked to cancer cell survival. 


Ankasha, Sheril June et al. “Oncogenic Role of miR-200c-3p in High-Grade Serous Ovarian Cancer Progression via Targeting the 3'-Untranslated Region of DLC1.” International journal of environmental research and public health vol. 18,11 5741. 27 May. 2021, doi:10.3390/ijerph18115741
Wang, J., Sun, J., Liu, L.N. et al. Siglec-15 as an immune suppressor and potential target for normalization cancer immunotherapy. Nat Med 25, 656–666 (2019). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41591-019-0374-x

Interesting top hits - gene expression

Interesting top hits

FGF7 - Epithelial growth factor; wound healing and cancer growth
IL6 - Pro-inflammatory cytokine; promotes tumor growth and treatment resistance
RAD9B - DNA damage checkpoint support
PLAC1 - immune evasion and EMT in HNSC
MAGEA1 - Cancer-testis antigen; proto-oncogene and immunotherapy target
LYVE1 - Lymphatic vessel receptor; metastasis marker
